title: Belton-in-Rutland & Northamptonshire (Naseby)

main_route.excerpt: We had coffee on the way at Nasby. Fairfax & Ireton won the battle against Prince Rupert.
We arrived at Belton-in-Rutland *** at 11.45. There is a pretty farm house with horses & portabloos. About 15 caravans.
After failing to get lunch at a village pub, we ate at a coaching inn in Uppingham & returned home in the sun.

excursions.label: Walk around Belton-in-Rutland village and church

stops.excerpt: walked up to look at the church. The Gough family gave a window to St. George. There is a face with donkey ears, sticking its tongue out in the piscina.

unresolved.issue: Place name uncertain; photo caption reads 'Sunny supper at Upper [?Cautres] after a very wet days' riding' — likely a village near Belton-in-Rutland visited on a cycling excursion, but exact location unclear. Could be Upper Caldecott or another local hamlet; '[?Cautres]' is a transcription guess.
unresolved.excerpt: Sunny supper at Upper [?Cautres] after a very wet days' riding

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page A_0152_R also contains a Sunday June 18 entry (Middleton Hall concert) that belongs to earlier diary content, not this camping trip
mixed_end: page A_0153_R is marked boundary_mixed; the Upper [?Cautres] photo caption may belong to a different outing
summary: A short weekend camping trip on 24–25 June 1995, leaving Birmingham via Naseby (battlefield coffee stop) to a campsite at Belton-in-Rutland, Rutland. The family walked the village, visited the church, had a barbecue, and returned home on Sunday via a coaching-inn lunch in Uppingham.
extraction_notes: Thomas is absent from the June 24 travellers list; Karoleia (a guest) is included instead. The Sunday June 18 Middleton Hall entry shares page A_0152_R but is not part of this camping trip — it is pre-trip mixed content. The photo caption 'Sunny supper at Upper [?Cautres] after a very wet days' riding' appears on the boundary page A_0153_R and may relate to a cycling excursion from the campsite; the place name is uncertain and marked unresolved.
